reasoning: RULE A applied: wrong tool for a broad 'tell me about Sado Island' query — get_japan_heritage scoped to Niigata heritage returns only heritage designations, not the general island overview intended. The two Sado Gold Mine entries (Q11248197, Q127378385, UNESCO WHS) are authoritative and directly relevant; one non-Sado Niigata item (笹山遺跡 ceramics) is included — factual but off-target, hence factual_accuracy 4. RULE C applied to intent/specificity/practical_usefulness: real authoritative data present but scope/filter gap → 3 each. lang:'ko' was correctly passed per RULE D; benefit of doubt on Korean field translations in truncated portion → constraint_handling 4. The browse_advisory nav-chrome block is very long and dominates visible output before actual data → expression_quality 3.
missing_data: General Sado Island spots (scenic, cultural, nature), transport/ferry access info, accommodation, local food, performing arts (Noh theater), and a broader island overview beyond heritage designations.
improvement_hint: For a broad island query, route to search_area(q='佐渡島') or get_spots(prefecture='Niigata', municipality='佐渡市') first. get_japan_heritage is appropriate as a supplementary call for heritage context. Also trim the browse_advisory block to reduce nav-chrome bulk.
